expectations.evidence: Report header: 'Method: Automated inspection via openpyxl (formula-level, no skill files used)'. No idfa_audit.py was used.

expectations.text: Reports status for Guardrail 1 (Named Range Priority) — should be PASS for this model
expectations.evidence: 'Named ranges: PASS. Every input and every calculated cell has a named range. Formulas use names exclusively — no raw cell references.'

expectations.text: Reports status for Guardrail 2 (LaTeX Verification) — PASS or WARN (no complex formulas like WACC)
expectations.evidence: No mention of LaTeX verification or Guardrail 2. The audit does not check for this.

expectations.text: Reports status for Guardrail 3 (Intent Notes) — should reflect comment coverage
expectations.evidence: No mention of intent notes/comments or Guardrail 3 in the audit. The report does not check for cell comments.

expectations.text: Reports status for Guardrail 4 (Layer Isolation) — should be PASS (no hardcoded values)
expectations.evidence: 'No hardcoded values in formulas: PASS. All numeric inputs live on the Assumptions sheet.'
Scoring Summary shows category scores totaling 'Overall: 33/50'. While not an IDFA compliance score per se, it does present an overall numeric assessment.

expectations.text: Explains what each guardrail checks in terms the user can understand
expectations.evidence: The report uses its own audit categories (Sheet architecture, Named ranges, Formula correctness, etc.) rather than IDFA guardrails. It does not explain IDFA guardrails.
